Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I filed a Paypal claim against a seller in Massachusetts from whom I purchased an item that was misrepresented to me. When I received the item, I contacted the seller to let her know that the item was not as she described and not acceptable, and that I wished a refund in accordance with her website 's stated refund policy. She promptly blocked me without any response. I made a claim to Paypal, and submitted numerous photos of the item I received along with screenshots of what was advertised, testimonials, and also informed them that the seller blocked me. This was XXXX XXXX. I sent the item back to the seller XXXX XXXX and it was delivered XXXX XXXX, and I provided proof of this. I expected a refund at that point. 

In the meantime, Paypal has continued to make repeated demands for photos, proof and tracking information, which I have provided multiple times. They also demanded at least three times, after I informed them the item was returned to the seller, that I provide an evaluation of the item from an independent party showing the item was not as described. And all this has been done under the threat that if I do not provide this information, they will decide the case in favor of the seller. 

First of all, as the buyer, I am the one who decides if an item is acceptable to me or not, as is my right as a customer. Secondly, I no longer have the item in my possession to bring to any independent party to make such an evaluation. I contacted Paypal last week, and the agent removed the request for an evaluation. They once again made a request for an independent evaluation this past week. I again contacted them and told them the item has been returned to the seller and delivered. I did attempt to get a third-party authenticator to at least look at the photos and try to render an opinion, but they informed me they were unable to do such an evaluation, and I have provided that communiqu to Paypal. And today, for a third time, Paypal asked for tracking information and for me to provide a third-party evaluation. 

There should be no more demands or discussion and they should just issue a refund at this point of the {$1500.00} I paid for an item that was not as it was advertised and not acceptable to me and has been returned. It is not as if I have retained the item in my possession and am demanding a refund. I would add that in addition to all the photos I took and provided to them, I also have video footage of the unboxing of the item in question and the repacking of it for shipment, which I have repeatedly offered to provide Paypal, but never received any answer from them. Instead of refunding, Paypal is being obstinate, hostile and unreasonable. Where is their buyer protection that they tout? This is also false advertising on their part. 

I would appreciate it if there is something you can do to help me recover my funds or can intervene in this matter because Paypal 's conduct here is egregious. 

Thank you in advance.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/year>2024 I received a phone call. The name that came up was XXXXXX/XX/XXXX ( I do not have an account there ). The person ( who spoke without an accent ) informed me that someone had tried to open an account with my information. Once I informed them it was not me, they then told me the person had hacked my XXXX account and was trying to make two transfers. 

In order to reverse these transfers, I had to follow his instructions which were to enter the amount and " send '' money ( he said that's not what I was doing, even though it seemed that way ) with a special code in the memo. The code was several numbers. I did this from both my checking and savings account and ended up losing {$2100.00}. This was just about everything I had at that time. I do not remember getting the " extra warning '' that I was sending to a new recipient, etc. 

It should be noted that at this time, I was a few days home from aXX/XX/XXXXXXXX and on major pain killers. The person on the line asked me if I had spent some time in the last week on public wifi, which I had in the hospital ( I believe they knew I had recently gotten out of the hospital ). 

I should have realized this was a scam immediately, but with the pain meds and the fact that I had indeed just spent much time on public wifi, I didn't catch it. But I realized as soon as I hung up. 

I went immediately to my bank ( Citizens ). They told me they would look into it but didn't think they would be able to help. I also filed a police report, about which I have heard nothing. I got a letter from Citizens stating that I had been denied and they would not return any of the money. 

I called, waited on hold for over and hour to speak to a " manager '' who rudely told me that I had authorized the transfer and despite the way the scammers played on me and despite my highly medicated state, they would re-evaluate, but again they didn't think they could help me. They said they would send me another letter with their decision, which they never did. I have heard nothing since and have given up on trying to recover any money. I am REALLY hoping you can help. Thank you.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX and XXXX of XXXX, I was a victim of fraudulent activity and theft on my Wells Fargo Savings and Checking Accounts that I did not authorize. Below are step-by-step occurrences of the following crimes committed. 

( XX/XX/XXXX ) My savings account of {$4000.00} was externally compromised. 

( XX/XX/XXXX ) The perpetrator fraudulently transferred {$4000.00} from my savings account - to my checking account. 

( XX/XX/XXXX ) My savings account was left with only {$0.00}. 

( XX/XX/XXXX XXXX ) The perpetrator then fraudulently made XXXX separate transactions through my checking account, resulting in a theft of {$3800.00} BELOW ARE ALL THE FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS MADE ON MY CHECKING ACCOUNT : -- {$1400.00} ( FRAUDULENT XXXX  TRANSACTION ON XX/XX/XXXX ) -- -- {$550.00} ( FRAUDULENT XXXX MONEY TRANSFER ON XX/XX/XXXX ) -- -- {$1000.00} ( FRAUDULENT XXXX MONEY TRANSFER ON XX/XX/XXXX ) -- -- {$890.00} ( FRAUDULENT XXXX MONEY TRANSFER ON XX/XX/XXXX ) -- On XX/XX/XXXX - XXXX received an unrecognizable package containing XXXX torn-up hats delivered to my address from XXXX ' that I never ordered or confirmed as an authorized purchase with Wells Fargo. 

After investigation, Wells Fargo determined 3 of the XXXX transactions to be fraud and refunded me the lost funds. Wells Fargo, however, denied the following claim and proceeded to reverse the credit of {$1400.00} from my account on ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : -- {$1400.00} ( FRAUDULENT XXXX  TRANSACTION ON XX/XX/XXXX ) -- Wells Fargo told me this claim is closed and that Im automatically responsible for the missing {$1400.00} dollars charged to my account due to the " product '' being delivered and the merchant having a " compelling case. '' After researching this company, the supposed website the " purchase '' was made from, did not exist. 

My main issue with Wells Fargo is how such a large transaction was made on my account in the first place without any contact from Wells Fargo via mobile app notification, text, or call. There were no security alerts. All transactions I make are always sent to me. These transactions, however, were not. My bank history has shown that I have never made such large transactions/purchases for Wells Fargo to approve of this transaction without reporting suspicious activity. 

I was given no opportunity to speak with Wells Fargo before they came to a final decision on my claim. When I asked for more details, I was told that it was an authorized transaction and that my claim was closed and could not be reopened. Wells Fargos lack of security/communication on this transaction, as well as providing information regarding more details of this transaction, where the transaction was made, how the transaction was made, or if a CVV code was used- is the cause for my complaint; forcing me to continue to take further action in resolving this crime committed against me. 

Wells Fargo determined the other transactions made on my account ( XXXX ) to be fraudulent ; however, they determined this particular transaction as legitimate ; even though this transaction was made on XX/XX/XXXX ( the same day my account was compromised. ) After further research, I found Wells Fargo was the victim of a data breach in XX/XX/XXXX ( when this theft occurred. ) However, Wells Fargo never acknowledged this. Instead, they made a final decision and closed my case. 


I hope Wells Fargo can come to an understanding so that we can further resolve this issue. 

As of XX/XX/XXXX : I have filed a complaint to XXXX ( Internet Crime Complaint Center ) I am in the process of filing a police report with the local police regarding this issue. 

I will also take any additional steps needed and will provide any information regarding this theft.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I am submitting this formal complaint regarding a wire transfer transaction that I initiated from my Citibank account, which resulted in financial loss due to potential fraud and mishandling by both Citibank and XXXX XXXX. 

Summary of the Issue On XX/XX/XXXX, I approved a wire transfer of {$11000.00} from my Citibank account to a recipient 's XXXX XXXX account. I mistakenly listed the beneficiary as XXXX XXXX instead of the intended payee 's company name. This resulted in the recipient being unable to claim the funds initially. 

Realizing that the transaction seemed suspicious, I promptly contacted Citibank the next business day which is XX/XX/XXXX to request a recall of the funds. Shortly thereafter, a representative from XXXX XXXX cantacted me first to ask about this transaction and confirmed that the {$11000.00} was indeed present in their system. I explaned the situation saying that I filed a fraud claim with Citibank and asked them not allow the payee withdraw the money and assured me that it would not be released to the recipient. Based on this assurance, I believed my money was secure. 

However, despite my proactive steps, I was shocked to learn on XX/XX/XXXX, that the recipient had withdrawn the full {$11000.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, without my authorization. I found that when I called Citibank to see the status of my recall and now the person at the wire department told me that she see the confirmation of withdrawn. When I contacted Citibank few minutes later to request trace or payment confirmation, another person at the same wire department told me that since money was gone, now I should be on my own to get this money back. XXXX XXXX, meanwhile, has refused to provide any details regarding the transaction, stating that I am not their customer. 

Concerns and Violations 1. Citibank 's Failure to Act Swiftly on the Recall Request I reported the issue the very next business day, yet Citibank failed to secure or recover my funds. They did not provide timely updates on the recall request. I called Citibank more than 20 times, talked with many differnet people at the fraud department and wire department and I only discovered the unauthorized withdrawal after two months.Even after the withdrawl, the did not even try to inform me. Their response was dismissive, stating that I am now solely responsible for the loss. 

2. XXXX XXXX 's Negligence in Allowing the Withdrawal A XXXX XXXX representative explicitly assured me that my funds would not be released, yet the money was withdrawn despite their acknowledgment of my concerns. 
XXXX XXXX has refused to provide me with any trace details or confirmation of payment, citing that I am not their customer, despite my funds being involved in their banking system. 

Request for Investigation and Resolution Given the mishandling of this transaction and the potential fraudulent activity, I am requesting : 1. A full investigation into Citibank 's and XXXX XXXX 's handling of this wire transfer, including their processes for fraud claims and recall requests. 
2. A detailed explanation of why Citibank failed to recall the funds in a timely manner despite my early intervention.

3. An inquiry into XXXX XXXX 's failure to prevent the withdrawal after acknowledging my concern, including their internal fraud prevention measures. 
4. Assistance in recovering my {$11000.00}, as both banks failed to act responsibly. 

I believe both Citibank and XXXX XXXX have acted negligently, and I request that they be held accountable for their actions. Please let me know the next steps regarding this complaint and any additional information you require. 

Thank you for your time and assistance in this matter. 

Sincerely,

Request for Action Given the severity of the situation, I request that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) investigate Robinhood for the following reasons : Failure to Provide Adequate Support : XXXX has not provided sufficient or timely responses to my inquiries and has disabled critical customer support channels. 
Unjust Account Restrictions : XXXX has imposed account restrictions without providing any legitimate explanation or justification, causing financial harm to its users. 
Failure to Resolve Ongoing Issues : Despite multiple attempts on my part to resolve this issue, XXXX has continued to push back the resolution timeline and has failed to take any meaningful action to restore access to my account. 
I am requesting that the CFPB take immediate action to ensure that XXXX resolves this issue, lifts the restrictions on my account, and compensates affected customers for the financial harm caused. Additionally, I urge the CFPB to investigate XXXX practices regarding account restrictions and customer support, as this issue may extend to a large number of other consumers.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at ( XXXX ) XXXX or my email XXXX should you need further details or documentation related to this complaint. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX ( known as XXXX at the time ) scammed me and 6 of my friends for a total of {$3700.00} for XXXX tickets that he never gave us. The transactions happened over XXXX and XXXX was able to convince most of us that he was trustworthy and even said directly that " he wouldn't scam us, he's not that kind of person. '' I met XXXX in XXXX of XXXX backstage at a music festival in XXXX XXXX and got his contact info because we made small talk and he seemed like a cool guy. Over the coming months, XXXX would send me occasional friendly messages and even wish me a happy birthday in XXXX. When XX/XX/XXXX came around, I made a story post on my XXXX that my friends and I were looking for XXXX tickets to buy from anyone who happened to be selling. XXXX then reached out to me claiming that he worked press for XXXX and that they gave them a certain number of last-minute festival tickets to help the festival sell. I instantly took up the deal, as it was nearing the festival ( which was about a week away ), and immediately hit up my friends who were also looking for tickets, referring them to him. Over the course of that day, XXXX and I got into group chats with my friends to facilitate the deals, promising that he would hand deliver the wristbands by that night. 

Come that night, XXXX fabricated a story about how he was picking up the wristbands and was headed over only for him to never show up and completely go ghost on text communication. I tried calling my bank ( JPMorgan Chase ), to which they said they were not liable for any transactions that were executed via XXXX willingly. The only reason I was because of a friend who reached out claiming that she knew the guy after I took to social media about the issue ( I am a content creator, so I have about XXXX people who follow me and could know the person ). From here, I was able to find XXXX XXXX mother on XXXX, to which I messaged her, telling her about the situation and asking her to tell her son to please get back into contact with us so we could sort the situation out. She cooperated and was able to get XXXX to begin communications back up with us, and he apologized and admitted openly to scamming us because he was desperate for money. 

However, when it came to getting this issue solved through our banks, Chase was " not actually able to do anything '' because the transaction had been willingly taken on me and my friends ' sides ( we initiated the payment ). Even when XXXX told the alleged banker ( I say alleged because he is the one who got me on the phone with this person, so I don't actually know if it was a legitimate banker or not ) that he had scammed us, the bank still said it was out of their control because most of the funds had already been withdrawn and, once we had reported XXXX ( after realizing what had been done ) they had frozen his bank account completely and told him that he could only receive his money by requesting a cashier 's check. Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I made several purchases from XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) using PayPal, relying on PayPals Buyer Protection Policy for security. When I encountered issues with my purchases, I followed PayPals dispute resolution process, but PayPal engaged in the following misconduct : 1. Facilitated Merchant Retaliation After filing a legitimate dispute, XXXX banned my account, preventing access to previous purchases. 
This was retaliation for using PayPals dispute system, and PayPal refused to intervene despite multiple reports.

2. Unfairly Denied My Dispute PayPal denied my claim, allowing the merchant to retain my funds while barring me from accessing my purchases.

This contradicts PayPals Buyer Protection Policy and violates 15 U.S.C. 1666 ( Fair Credit Billing Act ).

3. Allowed Harassment & Coercion G2G sent multiple emails pressuring me to drop the dispute in exchange for access to prior purchases.

PayPal refused to take action, violating 15 U.S.C. 45 ( FTC Act ), which prohibits unfair trade practices. 
4. Misrepresented Buyer Protection Policy PayPal falsely claimed that it does not intervene in merchant account bans, contradicting its own policy that buyers are entitled to a refund if they can not access their purchase.

This is false advertising and deceptive trade practices under 15 U.S.C. 45.

5. Pattern of Consumer Harm & Prior Violations PayPal has a documented history of shielding fraudulent merchants and denying valid consumer claims.

In 2015, PayPal was fined {$25.00} XXXX by the CFPB for deceptive dispute practices. 
This establishes a pattern of misconduct warranting regulatory enforcement. 

Legal Violations : PayPals actions constitute violations of multiple federal consumer protection laws, including : 15 U.S.C. 45 ( FTC Act ) Prohibiting unfair and deceptive trade practices.

15 U.S.C. 1666 ( Fair Credit Billing Act ) Requires financial intermediaries to fairly investigate disputes.

15 U.S.C. 1693 ( Electronic Fund Transfer Act ) Mandates fair transaction processing. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( 9th Cir. 2018 ) Payment processors can not enable merchant fraud. 
XXXX v. XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX. XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) Failure to enforce consumer protection policies is deceptive and unlawful. 

Requested CFPB Action : 1. Investigate PayPals Dispute Handling Practices Examine how PayPal denies valid disputes and enables merchant retaliation.

2. Issue Financial Penalties Fine PayPal under 15 U.S.C. 45 for deceptive practices.

3. Mandate Policy Reforms Require PayPal to prohibit seller retaliation, enforce fair disputes, and ensure policy compliance. 
4. Issue a Consumer Advisory Warning Warn consumers that PayPal fails to enforce Buyer Protection and allows merchants to retaliate against disputes.

Consumer complaint narrative:
On the afternoon of XX/XX/scrub>XXXX XXXX XXXX I closed on the sale of my home. Since it was completed in the afternoon, the funds wired for the closing were to hit my account on XX/XX/year>XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX the Originating Bank for the wire ) confirmed that the wire had been completed on Friday, XX/XX/year>, at XXXX, but I was not seeing the funds in my account at XXXX XXXX The details of the wire are as follows : XX/XX/year> Transaction # XXXX Amount : {>= $1,000,000} USD On Monday, XX/XX/year>, I still did not see the funds in my account, so I contacted my closing agent XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Title who sent me the confirmation she received from XXXX XXXX When I received it, I was completely confused. I told her that these were the wrong wire instructions. The wire was supposed to go to XXXX XXXX XXXX not to Citibank. She said that I had sent her an email over the weekend confirming the instructions to Citibank. To make a long story short, a hacker had gotten in between my emails to XXXX XXXX, my closing agent and I, and he diverted the funds from XXXX XXXX XXXX to an old XXXX account held at Citibank.. 


On Tuesday, my closing agent and I compared all the emails that we had sent between us, and we found that the bank wiring instructions had been changed. She immediately contacted XXXX XXXX who quickly tried to recall the wire from Citibank. She also contacted Citibank customer service and the Citibank Fraud Department to alert them of what had transpired. Later, she also contacted the FBI due to the amount of the wire. Later that day, the hacker acting as me asked her to change the For Further Credit To instructions to a company she had never heard of, but she refused to do so. 

During her conversation with Citibank Customer Service, the gentleman she spoke to confirmed the receipt of the funds into a XXXX account at Citibank on Friday, XX/XX/XXXX. He confirmed to her that the account had already been flagged numerous times for illegal activity and that the funds had NOT left the account. She asked and he promised to make sure that those funds never left the account. The bottom line is Citibank should have bounced this wire back to XXXX XXXX Because the wire transfers instruction did not perfectly match, specifically the For Further Credit To instructions which were : XXXX : Acct # XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX We waited 10 business days for the wire to be recalled to XXXX XXXX by Citibank, but Citibank refused to cancel and return the wire because they believed fraud had occurred and they needed to research the problem. 

It has now been over 30 days and Citibank has stonewalled everyone involved in the transaction failing to provide any information to anyone. They state that the problem is an XXXXXXXX issue and they will not provide any information to anyone but XXXX XXXX even though it is my money, and I am the victim. Now, they are not even getting back to XXXX XXXX when they ask for an update. I believe they are stalling because they screwed up. This wire should have initially been bounced back to XXXX XXXX and when it wasn't, it should have then been recalled back to XXXX XXXX in the first 5 to 10 business days after the transaction. Citibank doesn't even know its internal rules with regards to this transaction as I have called numerous times and was told different timeframes for completing their investigation. I have been told 30 days, 30 business days and 90 days. All of these timeframes are absurd as XXXX XXXX provided Citibank with all of the information for the wire Day XXXX. 

Will you please help me? I do not know how to proceed. No one will give me any information and I am in the dark. I am not a rich man, and these are my life savings. 

Thanks you. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, FL XXXX

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I am writing to formally request a further review and escalation of my complaint against Crypto.com ( Complaint ID XXXX XXXX  ). Crypto.coms response does not adequately address the concerns raised in my original complaint, and I believe that their handling of this matter has failed to provide appropriate consumer protection.

Key Issues with Crypto.coms Response : 1. Lack of Consumer Safeguards Against Fraudulent Transactions Crypto.com states that I voluntarily initiated the transactions to a third-party wallet ; however, they failed to implement adequate security measures to protect users from fraudulent transactions. 
- Crypto.com does not verify or flag potentially fraudulent wallets before processing transactions. 
- No warnings or risk alerts were provided when sending funds to an unknown or unverified external wallet. 
- Other financial institutions often have fraud prevention mechanisms, such as transaction holds, additional verifications, or fraud alertsCrypto.com failed to provide any such protections. 

2. Failure to Provide Assistance in Recovering Lost Funds Crypto.com claims they can not reverse blockchain transactions, but they have not taken any steps to assist in the recovery of my stolen funds. Possible actions they could take include : - Flagging the receiving wallet address as fraudulent to prevent further scams.

- Coordinating with law enforcement or blockchain forensic services to trace or freeze the funds.

- Contacting the exchange or service where the stolen assets were transferred to request a freeze or investigation.

3. Negligence in Educating and Protecting Users Against Fraud Risks - While Crypto.com provides educational resources, these are not actively enforced within the platform. They failed to warn me about common scams before allowing the transaction to proceed. 
- No real-time fraud detection system was used to flag my transaction despite it being an unusual or high-risk transfer. 

Request for Further Review and Action Given the above, I respectfully request that CFPB : - Escalate this case for further review and reconsider whether Crypto.coms consumer protection practices comply with financial safety regulations. 
- Require Crypto.com to provide a more detailed resolution regarding their due diligence, fraud prevention, and potential remediation steps. 
- Investigate whether Crypto.com should be held accountable for failing to provide adequate protections against fraud.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I was on XXXXXXXX XXXX looking to buy a computer. I reached out to someone on marketplace who was selling XXXX for {$1000.00} on XX/XX/year>, and he said i could pick it up or he could ship it. I was initially going to go pick it up, but then later asked to ship it. I didnt think it would be a scam if he was offering to pick it up, so I asked if he could ship it instead, since it would be far for me to pick up. 

On XX/XX/year> at XXXX and XXXX, he sent pictures of the computer packaged and also sent pictures of him at the XXXX store XXXX it out. He also sent a photo of a XXXX receipt in an email, which looked real to me, so I didnt question it at the time. On the receipt, it had my zip code and city, the sellers location, and said XXXX at XXXX by XXXX/XXXX/2925. I sent {$50.00} to the sellers Zelle at XXXX on XX/XX/year>, and then another {$1000.00} at XXXX on XX/XX/year>. However, after sending the money, the seller claimed that the {$1000.00} did not go through. We reasoned that it might take a few hours or days for the money to process since it was a large amount, and left it at that, despite my suspicions, since the money was withdrawn from my account already through Zelle. 

After, I kept checking the tracking number on the receipt to see when the item would ship. After it did not ship for 2 days, I reached out to the seller on XX/XX/year> at XXXX. He got back to me at XXXX and claimed he did not get the money, and asked me to call my bank and see where the money was. I asked him to call his bank and to forward the XXXX receipt to me as well, but he did not respond to me. 

On XX/XX/year>, I called XXXX at XXXX to ask about the package and tracking number, and they said the tracking number was missing XXXX digits. After this call, I looked online for fake XXXX receipts, and the first result matched the receipt that the seller sent me. The card details and transaction numbers on the fake receipt were exactly the same as the ones on the receipt the seller sent me. Because of this realization, I came to the conclusion that the photos he had sent me before must have been old photos or faked somehow. I also believe that the sellers roommate did not actually exist, and that the email he gave me was his own. This is because part of the email ID is present in XXXX of his social media names, where he goes by XXXX XXXX XXXX  and uses the username XXXX XXXX. 

After, I reported the seller on XXXX XXXX and contacted my bank at XXXX to file a claim. I did not reach back out to the seller again after that, as he had still not responded to my previous message asking him to forward me the XXXX receipt. 

On XX/XX/year>, I heard back from my banking institution, in which they decided the transaction was not fraud and considered the matter resolved. I called my bank back on XX/XX/year> to explain the situation and in the hope to appeal their decision. I was able to submit an appeal to my bank, in which I claimed the issue as a scam rather than a fraud. On XX/XX/year>, they said they would investigate the issue, and on XX/XX/year>, I heard back from them on their decision. My bank said that because I had authorized the transaction, and I had listed the payment as for goods and services on Zelle, they were unable to recover the payment and considered the dispute resolved. However, I was still unsatisfied with this resolution, as I had wrongfully been scammed out of a large sum of money, and the perpetrator was seemingly facing no repercussions.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative: I received a text message from Bank of America asking if I attempted to make a purchase for {$170.00} Krogers with my credit card on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX. At the same time, I received a phone call from XXXX from a person named " XXXX '' who claimed to be a bank of America associate. He stated my card had been charged with two transactions and that they were calling to verify that they were fraud since they were suspicious. He proceeded to tell me they were charges in Arizona, and he could see that I am a Maryland resident. While claiming to cancel the charges on my credit card which he knew the last 4 digits to, he then asked if I knew a XXXX XXXX. He asked if I was trying to wire her {$5500.00}. I told him no, that is not me and must be a scam. He then told me in order to stop the wire transaction from going through I had 20 minutes to dispute this with a wire reversal. I was uncomfortable so I asked how I was to be sure this was a bank of America. He then proceeded to give me my name, address, phone number, last 4 of account number and last 4 of my social security number. I hung up on him because I was worried and attempted to call bank of America on my own. He called back and said I could call the main line but by that time the wire would have gone through and could not be canceled. When googling canceling wire transfers it stated you had to do it in 20 minutes. He then proceeded to tell me I would likely be on hold with Bank of America for at least 40 minutes and would miss my chance to cancel the wire. I had just called bank of America the week prior and was actually given a hold time of 40 minutes. So everything he said was adding up. I then asked to speak to a manager to ensure this was bank of America. He then transferred me to a man who claimed to be XXXX XXXX. They said all the right things " these calls are being monitored and recorded '' throughout the phone call and would periodically give me the time I had left to do the wire reversal ( within the 20 minutes ). He walked me though step by step on my account and informed me that I was submitting a wire reversal. He had me put all my information in as the receiver, so I thought this was legit. I even questioned why I had to do this, and he said it had to come from my account since it was a wire transfer to cancel it. I submitted the request. He said he would call tomorrow to follow up about the account. I copied down the account number and routing number that I was given. After I was so upset about being hacked on my accounts, I decided to call Bank of America to see how this could have happened. I wanted to take additional steps to protect my account. I called 3 hours after at XXXXpm and the bank of America representative informed me that it was a scam and that they didn't call me. She assured me that she would file everything right away but that I needed to fill out a document and return it to them as soon as possible to avoid any delay in my case. She said I would have the document within 24 hours. I was told that I should have known it was a scam because " Bank of America will never call you ''. She also stated she would send all new credit cards since these accounts had been compromised. I locked all my bank of America credit cards on my app as well for safety. I immediately called XXXX XXXX to request the money be returned to my account, but they stated they could not help me and that my bank would need to contact them. I told them my bank was contacting them and asked if they could freeze the account until investigation. They stated they couldn't do that and the only thing I could do was submit through my bank for them to do anything on their end. After I notified my phone company to see how this happened and if I was hacked. The company stated that because I have an iPhone, that is the most secure phone you can have, and it is unlikely that there was any malware on my phone ( which is what the bank of America representative told me to do when I asked about how this happened ). I then filed a local police report, a report with the FTC, IC3 and local FBI of my stolen money. Because this person had all of my information, I contacted the credit bureaus and locked my information. I also got a pin for my taxes. I changed all my passwords. I called the bank of America the next day when I didn't receive the letter. They said they would send it again and give it 24 hours. That next day there was still nothing in my email, so I called again. This time I had them change my email thinking it was maybe my email address and that is why it wasn't coming through. I never got the letter. I called the following day and spoke to someone in the fraud department who advised me to go to a local branch and they could send the document to the branch. I went immediately to the branch and filled out the hold harmless letter. I was told I had to fill this out in order for my wire reversal request to be sent to XXXX XXXX and that a delay could be the difference in not getting my money back. I called almost every day for 3 weeks to get updates about my claim. I was told so many different things by different representatives. One representative I spoke to told me they try to resolve these cases quickly and that I should have an answer within 2 weeks and since it took them the rest of the week to get me the hold harmless document I was almost there. I called back and spoke to someone 1 or 2 days later and they told me that it could take 45 days but that they were waiting on a response from XXXX XXXX. I was told I would receive an email and the money back to my account if there were funds in the account. I asked if there was anything Bank of America could do to help with the financial hardship while we were waiting for the response. I was told to call local charities, and that bank of America couldn't do anything more to help. I asked if the {$30.00} transaction fee could be refunded while we waited for XXXX XXXX and was told no because it was a part of the claim. I was told when the claim was resolved I would be able to get that fee returned. I called a few days later again and was told that my case had been assigned to an analyst that was " most likely tracking the money because sometimes these scammers will transfer the money out of the account to another account ''. I was told I needed to give it more time. I called back again a few days later to get an update and was told no updates. I called back a few days later and was told that my claim status changed to collection so that was good sign of me getting my money back. They said that meant that XXXX XXXX received the information, and they were again " most likely tracking the money ''. I was told if there was any money in the account the bank would return it but that there were no guarantees that there was any money left in the account. I called back and asked about the information at the bottom of my wire transaction online that states most wires could take between 1-2 business days before they are received by your recipient. I asked the representative about that because prior I was told wires are immediate. He stated that it depends on the type of wire transaction domestic vs international, if you have ever wired money to this person before and what the amount is for. He said for security purposes those take a little longer. I told him that was good then because if they didn't release the funds, I would still have a chance in getting my money back. He said yes because I called within 3 hours of the wire, I have a really good chance of getting my money back. He even said that if the person scammed more than one person and the account was already frozen it is very likely I will get my money back and he has seen people get their money back before. I called back a few days later and was told the same thing but that sometimes this process takes some time because XXXX XXXX might have to request the money from another bank if my money was transferred from that account to an account outside of XXXX XXXX. I called again a few days later and spoke to someone who said it could take up to 90 days and that XXXX XXXX could not respond back to Bank of America. They said in case of no response that they would reach back out for another 45 days. They said after 90 days with no response the claim would be declined as unsuccessful. On 2/6 received the SAME thing text message, phone call and email from Bank of America asking about the card with the SAME LAST four of the account number as before. The text asked if I attempted to make a purchase to XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX  for {$56.00} and that it was declined. This time I did not answer the phone. I blocked everything and called the bank of America fraud department directly. The bank of America rep stated " Oh, ma'am this is actually us trying to get a hold of you this time ''. I was stunned. I told the representative that before with the last fraud I was told " bank of America would NEVER call '' but now you are saying they will?! How was I supposed to know it was not the real bank of America last time since the same thing happened last time and I was robbed {$5500.00}. He apologized and stated this charge was all taken care of and I didn't have to worry. I asked how this was ever possible since when it happened the first time this was the same last 4 of the card number before and I was told I would be getting all new cards to ensure my safety. He stated he didn't see where I was issued new cards and that I would have to do that again. So, he requested, and I requested all new cards, and I then locked my credit cards again. I asked if this fraud could be reported to my analyst since it was the same thing that happened before but this time actual charges. I asked him how this was possible since I made the request before and how it was possible since I never lost possession of that specific card. He stated it could have been hacked from an online retailer, but I just got the card last year and only used it a handful of times. I called back the next day and spoke to XXXX to make sure the information got to my analyst and that notes were made on my account. I was told they were and that this charge was already declined and there was nothing further I needed to do. I called again on XX/XX/XXXX and they said there should be a date listed behind the processing status. They stated they would send a message to the " back office '' and to call back tomorrow or the next day to follow up. I called back XX/XX/XXXX and they stated THEY HAD NO NOTES ON MY ACCOUNT ABOUT MY PREVIOUS PHONE CALL ON XX/XX/XXXX. They told me to give it more time and that it takes 45 business days before they could do anything additional for my case. I told them about all the misinformation I received and how I don't trust Bank of America and feel like I am being lied to. Why was I calling and getting different answers every single day? Why were there no notes on the account when the representative said she would make notes specifically and instructed me to call back. Was anything they were saying true? Did they even contact XXXX XXXX? How did this happen in the first place? Did bank of America leak my information or did an old employee that had access to my information call me on the XX/XX/XXXX and steal my money. What is actually happening. I googled to see if Bank of America had any consumer data leaked and sure enough, they did in XXXX of XXXX. XXXX of the third parties that Bank of America shares your consumer data with was hacked. I called that same day to see if my information was leaked in that data leak. I was told that I would have received something in the mail, and they couldn't see anything about the data leak I was talking about. After that day I decided to give it the full 45 days before calling back to follow up. They told me I would get an email if they heard back and I would see the money in my account. I was told if they didn't get a response by 45 business days that they would reach back out to XXXX XXXX and allow another 45 business days. I was told after 90 business days again my claim would be closed if I didn't hear back. I called today XX/XX/XXXX and spoke with XXXX from the XXXX DE office who stated that my claim was closed, and they were unsuccessful and retrieving the funds from XXXX XXXX. I asked what that meant exactly, did they get a response from XXXX XXXX, if so, what did they say. He stated he could not tell me any more information other than that. I asked if XXXX XXXX responded and if not why bank of America was not reaching back out like they said they would multiple times and he stated that is not possible. I AM FLOORED. I told him about all the misinformation and demanded more information about the claim. He stated there was nothing more Bank of America could do for me. I asked multiple times to speak to a manager or someone above him. He stated that there was no one else and the only thing I could do was call the same number back but that I would get the same type of representative that was only allowed to give the same information. I asked why I didn't get an email like I was told, and he said that they sent a letter out in the mail XX/XX/XXXX which was last Friday. I should have already received the letter and still have not. I asked if there was a number at the bottom of the letter, I could call to get a hold of management, or a supervisor and he stated again it was the same number that would get back to his department level. I went off about how unacceptable this is for Bank of America to treat their costumers like this. I have been with Bank of America for 15 years. I told him how this is a crazy amount of money to me and people kill themselves for less and this is how bank of America chooses to treat their people! He then told me he misunderstood and that he was trying to say I couldn't speak to an analyst but that he could get a manager on the line. I told him that I knew from before I couldn't speak to an analyst. He asked me to wait while he got a manager on the phone. After about 15-20 minutes of waiting for a manager, one never came. XXXX told me that his manager gave him an email for me to write to with my complaints. The email is XXXX. I said to him this is insane, so your manager is refusing to speak to me. He stated he couldn't say anything more but direct me to send an email. I asked for the manager 's name, and he said he couldn't give it to me. UNREAL. I then requested all my notes, phone calls and documentation to be sent to me. He asked if I wanted it via email or mail. I told him how I never got any of the emails they claimed to send so please send it via the mail. I still haven't received this letter he states that was sent Friday. It has been a week. It should have been in the mail. I did the math so Friday would have been exactly 45 " business days '' from XX/XX/XXXX when the claim happened. So, most likely Bank of America just closed the claim and did not follow their promised process of resending documentation for another 45 business days. It is insane to me that XXXX XXXX can just not respond! It is also insanity to me that I can't trust a bank that I have been with for 15 years. HOW DO I EVEN KNOW THEY SUBMITTED THE REQUEST?! How do I know they did anything they said they were going to do? How do I know Bank of America isn't responsible for my information getting leaked and how do I know it really wasn't Bank of America that called me and had me transfer {$5500.00} out of my account? I have NEVER wired money from my bank of America account. Why didn't they stop that as suspicious activity? I have been a bank of America customer for 15 years and never had this happen. I have multiple different accounts with different banks and never had someone call impersonating my bank. When I checked CFPB 's site, Bank of America has 336 cases for fraud from the last 6 months and over 10 of the cases that were public are exactly like my claim. They also received calls and texts from bank of America impersonators. They suffered the SAME HUGE LOSSES from wire transfers. Bank of America made them the SAME PROMISES they made me. Told me the same things which turned out to be all lies. Why if Bank of America is doing the right thing for their customers, why haven't they started putting extra security tactics on their wire transfers. It is crazy to me because the dates that the claims from the others on this site are all around the SAME date as my fraud. That is crazy. I looked at other banks and none of them have the same thing of getting calls from their bank impersonators.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
In XX/XX/year>, I obtained a cashiers check from Chase Bank for {$80000.00}, made payable to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX as part of a business purchase. I later discovered that this entity was legally forfeited by the Maryland State Department of Assessments and Taxation ( SDAT ) at the time the check was issued and deposited. 

Further investigation also revealed that the forfeited LLC had a significant state tax lien, making it ineligible for reinstatement. This means the business I believed I was purchasing could not legally exist or operate, even if reinstatement were attempted. Despite this, XXXX XXXX  accepted and deposited the check into an account that should have been closed since XXXX issue XXXX has since acknowledged directly. 

XXXX has stated they are willing to issue a refund but require Chase to submit a formal claim or hold harmless agreement. However, Chase has refused to take this step, even though doing so would not involve refunding funds from their own accounts, but merely submitting routine documentation to allow the refund to proceed. 

This transaction should never have gone through. If either bank had followed standard compliance protocolsand the check had been rejected as it should have beenI would have known the business was not legally operational, and the transaction would have never occurred. Their failure to flag or reject this check directly enabled a fraudulent sale.

I am now left with nothing. I do not own the business, I received no value in return, and I am being denied access to the funds because the banks involved will not take basic corrective action. 

This is not a private civil matter. It is a serious breakdown in compliance and due diligence under Know Your Customer ( KYC ) and Anti-Money Laundering ( AML ) regulations. Both banks failed to prevent the transfer of funds to a forfeited and non-reinstatable entityviolating state and federal financial protections. 

The transaction also violates Maryland Corporations & Associations Code 4A-920, which deems transactions with forfeited entities void. Chases continued refusal to cooperate is sustaining a regulatory breach and obstructing recovery of clearly mishandled funds.

I request the CFPBs intervention to compel Chase to provide the required documentation to XXXX XXXX so the refund can be processed. The longer this continues, the more harm it causes and the more serious the compliance failure becomes.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/year> I got a text Wells Fargo Alert : Did you attempt a purchase at XXXX XXXX with card ending in XXXX for {$250.00} in XXXX Texas? Reply YES or NO. Reply STOP to stop msgs. 

I replied NO Wells Fargo Alert : Transaction declined. No further action required.A representative will reach out to you shortly.

A man called almost immediately and said he was with Wells Fargo fraud department. He asked me if I had made a purchase at XXXX for {$44.00}? I said yes, a purchase yo XXXX XXXX for {$43.00}, I said yes. A purchase to XXXX XXXX for {$27.00}, I said yes. 

He then asked if I took out a {$10000.00} withdrawal I said NO!! 

He said that my XXXX XXXX was set up incorrectly. I had just started using my XXXX XXXX a couple of weeks prior, so it sounded legitimate to me. 

He told me my XXXX XXXX account was set up wrong, and he would help me set up a secure account. He said he closed out my current debit card, and a new one would be issued is 5-7 days. 

He needed the code that was just sent to me to start the process. I gave him the code. He needed the second and third codes also that were sent to me. I gave that to him! 

He said my {$10000.00} is protected by the FDIC so I wont lose my money. He then said he will put {$10000.00} in discretionary funds into my XXXX XXXX, so I wont be short on money until this gets settled. 

He then asked me if I took out {$9000.00}?? I said NO!! He told me to go to my local Wells Fargo branch and take out {$9000.00}, so the scammer doesnt get more of my money. 

He said the local branch is being investigated because there are several others this has happened to, and it is a worker inside the bank giving out customers information! So dont tell the Teller about the investigation.

He told me to keep him on the line and put my phone in my pocket and let him know when the {$9000.00} transaction was complete. ( I put my phone on the Tellers counter ) I asked the Teller to withdraw {$9000.00} from my checking account. When she asked what for, I told her shopping and travel. She asked me if I had transferred money from my Savings account into checking, and I said no! She asked if I had made a large withdrawal that day, and I said no! She noticed my phone and asked who was on the line. I whispered to her a Wells Fargo fraud alert guy.

She said I was being scammed! He hung up immediately. 

I called the REAL Wells Fargo fraud company immediately while I was at the bank and met with the banker XXXX XXXX XXXX savings, checking and debit card was closed immediately. I filed a claim with Wells Fargo immediately. The banker XXXX said that I didnt need to file a police report! 

There was a {$10000.00} pending charge coming out of my checking account. That {$10000.00} came out of my Wells Fargo checking account 5 days later! 

I got notification on XX/XX/XXXX that Wells Fargo denied my claim. I went back to the banker XXXX XXXX And filed a complaint against my denied claim. I then filed a police report, and the officer said it is a Felony.

Wells Fargo said that I gave permission to have all my savings transferred to my checking account, and that I upped by daily debit card limit to {$16000.00}, and also gave permission to have the {$10000.00} taken out of my account!!! It was the scammer who did all that!! It was probably done with those codes he needed to set up apple pay I got notification today, that Wells Fargo denied my complaint appeal and said my claim is closed! I asked her If you had {$10000.00} fraudulently taken out of your account, would you just accept the claim being closed? She didnt really have an answer to that! 

I asked the claims department why stop payment wasnt done immediately on my {$10000.00} pending payment, when I had my accounts closed almost immediately. I also asked her where is my protection, keeping my money in a Wells Fargo bank. I told her that I thought Wells Fargo had insurance to protect their customers! She didnt really have an answer to that. 

I let the Wells Fargo claims/complaint department know that I will keep fighting to get my money back.

Background : Date of Sale : XX/XX/XXXX Item Sold : A custom-built musical instrument ( guitar ) Amount : {$740.00} USD Buyer : [ Buyer 's Name, if known ] Platform : Direct sale, payment made via PayPal Shipping Confirmed XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Item Received by Buyer : XX/XX/XXXX I provided the buyer with full disclosure of the item, including high-resolution photos and video footage showing the instrument being played, ensuring the buyer knew exactly what they were receiving. The buyer received the item, then immediately filed a dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, claiming the guitar was a " XXXX product '' and not a XXXX-built custom item which is categorically false. The instrument I sold was built in XXXX, long before the emergence of any overseas replicas. 

PayPal sided with the buyer and refunded them the {$740.00}. Critically, PayPal did not require the buyer to return the item. I now have neither my property nor the funds I am owed effectively resulting in theft, facilitated and enforced by PayPals system.

Compounding this issue, PayPal simultaneously and arbitrarily limited my account, alleging high risk due to shared account information with other accounts ( which I dispute ). As a result, they stripped me of Seller Protection eligibility after I had already completed the transaction and fulfilled every obligation as a seller. 

Efforts to Resolve : I provided proof of shipping, documentation of the sale, and a police report ( which I filed after the buyer refused to return the item ). 

I submitted multiple appeals through PayPals channels.

I contacted PayPal 's Office of Executive Escalation and received responses confirming that they would not change their decision. 

I also contacted the XXXX XXXX XXXX PayPal responded but stood by their decision. 

Unfair Treatment and Financial Loss : I upheld all obligations as a seller. 

The buyer filed a baseless claim and was rewarded with both the item and a full refund. 

PayPal denied me any form of protection or restitution, citing risk-level claims unrelated to this transaction. 

My account has since been permanently limited, despite providing all documentation requested. 

Relief Requested : I respectfully request that PayPal be required to : Reimburse me the {$740.00} I am owed for the item, or recover the item from the buyer.

Explain why the buyer was allowed to keep the item without returning it.

Clarify why Seller Protection was revoked retroactively and how this aligns with fair trade practices. 

Re-evaluate the arbitrary limitation placed on my account or provide transparency about the data used to make that determination. 

I believe PayPal failed to apply their User Agreement fairly, allowed a buyer to exploit the system, and failed to protect me from an evident case of abuse or fraud. Their refusal to review the matter further only exacerbates the harm caused. 

Please contact me if further documentation is needed, including the police report, correspondence with PayPal, or shipping evidence. 

Thank you for your time and assistance. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
XXXX XXXX XXXX I was the victim of a sophisticated scam by someone impersonating an XXXX XXXX representative. It started with a text message that looked like a legitimate fraud alert from XXXX asking whether I had attempted an XXXX XXXX transaction. When I responded No, I received a phone call from someone claiming to be with Allys fraud department. They were professional, used banking language, and gave me detailed instructions that I believed were helping me protect my account.
Following their directions, I sent two payments through my PayPal account totaling XXXX to someone named XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX at XXXX. I now know that I was being scammed. The impersonator specifically instructed me to mark the transactions as Sending to a friend, which I later found out removes PayPals standard purchase protection. I selected that option only because I believed I was working with XXXX XXXX to stop fraud, not because I was sending money to a friend or family member.
Although I technically authorized the transactions, that authorization was based entirely on lies and manipulation. I did not knowingly or willingly agree to send money to a scammer. I acted in good faith under false pretenses, which is not genuine consent under consumer protection law.
As soon as I realized what had happened, I contacted PayPal. The transaction was still pending, and I explicitly told them I had been scammed and asked them not to let the payment go through. I have a record of that communication. Despite this, PayPal refused to intervene, citing their policy that personal payments are not covered under Purchase Protection. That response ignored the full context and failed to take any action to stop fraud, even when I reported it in real time.
A few weeks later, the same scammer called me again and attempted the same scheme. This time, I recorded the conversation as evidence. I also saved the original scam text message, the email thread with PayPal, and confirmation of my reports to both the XXXX  Police Department (Case Number XXXX XXXX) and the Federal Trade Commission.
I have also filed a CFPB complaint against XXXX XXXX, which refunded two smaller charges made during the same time period. That refund confirms that my account was compromised. I am now asking the CFPB to hold PayPal accountable as well. PayPal allowed this scam to succeed and ignored my attempts to stop it, even though they had the tools and the notice to act.
This was not a friendly payment mistake. This was fraud by deception. I did everything I was supposed to do, and I still lost money. I am asking for help to make this right.'

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
Dear CFPB, I am submitting this escalated rebuttal related to my previous complaint ( XXXX XXXX : XXXX ) because PayPals response remains inadequate, unfair, and obstructive. 

PayPal stated that the issue was due to the senders bank, yet they are still restricting my account and demanding that I pay money in order to use my account again. While they agreed to remove the collection record, their demand for payment contradicts their own explanation and places an unreasonable burden on me. 

Additionally, after receiving PayPals explanation by phone ( XX/XX/2025 XXXX XXXX ), I attempted to call them back to rebut their response. However, my calls were automatically redirected to an automated collections message, preventing me from speaking with a representative or meaningfully disputing their claims. 

As a non-native XXXX speaker, I was unable to effectively rebut their statements during the initial phone call. Despite the unfair nature of their explanation, I had no choice but to remain silent and listen, which created an even greater imbalance and deprived me of a fair opportunity to dispute their position. 

My concerns are as follows : 1. Contradictory position : If this issue is truly caused by the senders bank, PayPal has no legitimate basis to hold me responsible or require payment for account reinstatement.

2. Unlawful restriction : PayPals refusal to restore my account without payment effectively punishes me for a situation outside of my control.

3. Lack of transparency : PayPal has not provided any documentation from the senders bank, nor a clear legal basis for their demand. 
4. Obstruction of dispute rights : Forwarding my calls to an automated collections message prevents me from exercising my right to dispute their response and seek resolution.

5. Language disadvantage : As a non-native XXXX speaker, I was placed at an even greater disadvantage and denied a fair chance to properly dispute PayPals claims. 
6. Potential coercion : Demanding payment in exchange for restoring access to my account, while admitting that I am not at fault, appears to be coercive and unfair.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
Bank of America XXXX XXXX XXXX : XXXX, XXXX, XXXX On XX/XX/year>, I became the victim of a sophisticated criminal operation in XXXX, XXXX. Me and my friend were drugged and rendered unconscious by a criminal gang who then systematically stole my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX laptop, and multiple credit cards while we were incapacitated. Upon regaining consciousness, I immediately filed an official police report with Colombian authorities documenting the theft, which has also been shared with Bank of America. 

While I was unconscious and after the theft, these criminals hacked my stolen phone and laptop to gain unauthorized access to my online banking accounts. Between XXXX XXXX XXXX fraudulent wire transfers and XXXX payments XXXX {$25000.00} were conducted from my Bank of America XXXX XXXX unknown international recipients. The criminals used my stolen devices and intercepted authorization codes sent by the bank through my compromised phone, making the transactions appear legitimate to Bank of America 's systems. 

Actions I took : - Filed Colombian police report immediately on XX/XX/year>, documenting all stolen items - Had no way to access my accounts or discover the fraud until returning to the XXXX, as my devices were stolen - Reported the fraud to Bank of America immediately upon discovering the unauthorized transfers - Filed formal fraud claims on XX/XX/year>, through my local branch - Submitted comprehensive additional evidence on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year>, requesting claim reopening after initial claim denial by Bank of America - Provided documentation from XXXX other affected financial institutions showing the same criminal incident Bank of America 's response : - Initially denied all fraud claims, stating my stolen XXXX was an " authorized device '' - Failed to acknowledge that the device was documented as stolen and hacked by criminals - Currently reviewing additional evidence after I provided overwhelming proof from multiple sources- Has not reversed any of the {$25000.00} in clearly fraudulent transactions Independent verification from other institutions : XXXX major financial institutions immediately recognized and responded to the same criminal incident : - Capital XXXX : Refunded fraudulent charges of {$750.00}, issued emergency replacement cards- XXXX XXXX : Immediately reversed fraudulent transactions of approximately {$4500.00} from June 29-30, 2025, issued new cards - XXXX XXXX : Blocked fraudulent attempts, issued replacement card due to documented theft - AT & T : Recognized device compromise and took protective measures. Issued new replacement phone. 

All these institutions processed fraud claims within days and fully refunded customers, recognizing the clear criminal activity. 

The fundamental problem : Bank of America 's position defies logic and contradicts documented evidence. They consider a device " authorized '' despite : - Official police report documenting it as stolen- The device being physically separated from me in a different country- XXXX other major financial institutions independently confirming the same criminal incident - Clear evidence that criminals had hacked the XXXX XXXX Bank of America 's fraud detection failed to flag highly suspicious activity : large wire transfers to unknown international recipients, conducted during foreign travel, from a customer with very less prior wire transfer history, using a stolen device that kept generating short messages active in XXXX after my departure. If they want, Bank of America 's own IT systems can easily verify through IP tracking that the stolen devices continued operating in XXXX while I was physically present in the United States. 

Impact on my family : As a XXXX Bank of America XXXX XXXX XXXX trusted this institution with my family 's financial security. The stolen {$25000.00} represents years of careful savings set aside for my children 's education. While other financial institutions that I am with immediately protected their customers and reversed fraudulent charges from this same criminal incident, Bank of America has left me and my family bearing the financial burden of a violent crime that I barely survived. 

Despite overwhelming evidence, documented police reports, and unanimous recognition of fraud by peer institutions, Bank of America continues to deny responsibility for transactions that were clearly conducted by criminals using stolen, hacked devices. Bank of America 's fundamental error is conflating device " authentication '' with customer " authorization. '' While the stolen phone XXXX have technically authenticated to their systems using saved credentials and intercepted codes, this does not constitute legitimate customer authorization when the device was documented as stolen and in criminal hands. A stolen device accessing an account through compromised credentials represents fraud, not authorized transactions, regardless of whether the device passes technical authentication protocols.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
At all times related to this claim, I was an accountholder at a United States-based cryptocurrency exchange and licensed Money Services Business ( MSB ) Coinbase , Inc. , ( Coinbase ) and held in my account/wallet valuable cryptocurrency assets. Coinbase promotes itself as The most trusted cryptocurrency platform that is guided by Industry best practices and that Cryptocurrency stored on our servers is covered by our insurance policy. I was aware of and relied on those representations of security in storing my assets on Coinbases platform. Despite its self-promotion about how secure, reliable, and trustworthy its services are, though, customers assets at Coinbase are at risk of being fraudulently withdrawn -- something of which Coinbase is well aware but has not taken adequate steps to prevent or remedy. 



Coinbase has had announced and unannounced breaches of data maintained on, and security of, its platform; leading to a variety of harms suffered by Coinbase account/wallet holders : support scams, penetrations of Coinbases platform leading to SIM swaps, failure to block blacklisted destination addresses, and account takeovers. Coinbase is responsible for securing its platform and all internal and external cryptocurrency payment rails, which serve as the infrastructure that facilitates the transfer of digital currencies between parties. In this case, Coinbase failed to secure data maintained on its platform and failed to properly verify the authenticity and integrity of the cryptocurrency payment rail ( s ) that transferred cryptocurrency from my account/wallet to the destination address of the recipient of my stolen assets. Coinbases failure to design and implement adequate security measures on its platform and on the payment rails was a material factor in directly and/or proximately causing my loss. Commencing on or about XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, Coinbases falsely advertised, faulty security measures failed me ; and as a result, I suffered the loss of cryptocurrency from my account/wallet that was valued at the time at approximately {>= $1,000,000}. 



I seek, among other things, restitution, compensatory damages, and equitable relief for the harm I have suffered, as I hold Coinbase accountable for platform breakdowns, failures in security design or implementation, and statutory/common law breaches that permitted/assisted the theft of the personal assets I held at Coinbase.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I placed an order with XXXX  for their XXXX flashlight ( XXXX lumens ) and rechargeable lithium-ion battery, totaling {$150.00}, paid via Cash App. What I received was not the product advertised, and the shipment raised serious concerns about safety, misrepresentation, and environmental compliance. 

Instead of the XXXX  XXXX, I received a generic, unbranded flashlight labeled XXXX XXXX that is non-functional, incompatible with the battery, and lacks internal contact springs. The lithium-ion battery was shipped without proper insulation or compliant packaging, violating U.S. Department of Transportation ( DOT ) and International Air Transport Association ( IATA ) regulations for hazardous materials. The parcel was routed through third-party handlers and bore a shipping label referencing a completely unrelated recipient and address.

USPS tracking confirmed that the package was held due to unpaid postage, and the delivery address did not match the one I provided. This constitutes a clear fulfillment error, and the merchant has refused to issue a refund despite multiple requests and documentation. 

I submitted a formal dispute and appeal to Cash App, including : Photos of the incorrect flashlight and battery USPS tracking logs showing unpaid postage and delivery failure Email correspondence with XXXX  confirming the item was not the XXXX Evidence of unsafe packaging and misrouting Despite this, Cash App denied my appeal. This decision contradicts their Buyer Protection Policy, which covers items not received, not as described, and unsafe goods. 

This transaction violates multiple federal laws and consumer protections : 15 U.S. Code 45 Deceptive practices under the Federal Trade Commission Act 16 CFR 238.2 False advertising and misrepresentation Consumer Product Safety Act ( CPSA ) Unsafe product delivery and failure to meet safety standards DOT/IATA Lithium Battery Shipping Regulations Improper packaging of hazardous materials USPS Parcel Handling Regulations Misrouted and unpaid postage As a consumer, I feel deeply betrayed. I paid for a premium product and received a dangerous, misrepresented item. Ive exhausted all reasonable channels to resolve this, and I am now turning to the CFPB for intervention.
