Consumer complaint narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, My name is XXXX XXXX, and I am a XXXX  XXXX in XXXX  XXXX, Colorado. I am filing a formal complaint against PayPal XXXX XXXX regarding a significant mishandling of a dispute involving funds paid for merchandise that was never delivered. 

I operate multiple retail businesses that rely on PayPal for purchasing inventory and conducting customer transactions. I have maintained a long, clean, and consistent business relationship with PayPal for many years. 



Summary of the Issue In XXXX, I placed an order with a seller in XXXX for a shipment of crystals used for retail sale in my store. I made XXXX separate payments to this seller through PayPal : {$2100.00} USD {$3100.00} USD Both payments were for the same order from the same seller. 

The seller did not ship the merchandise I paid for. Instead, he sent a small, low-value package weighing only a few ounces, despite the fact that the actual order should have been approximately XXXX pounds of XXXX material. The seller even admitted to me through messages that he had not sent out the packages and that the real shipment was still sitting on his desk. 

I filed disputes with PayPal for both transactions. 
PayPal approved the {$2100.00} dispute. 
PayPal denied the {$3100.00} dispute, allowing the scammer to keep the funds. 

This denial occurred despite both transactions involving the same seller, the same fraudulent behavior, the same evidence, and the same violation of XXXX XXXX XXXX policies. 



Actions I Have Taken I have spent weeks attempting to resolve this directly with PayPal : I contacted PayPal customer support multiple times. 
I submitted all evidence, including : Photos of the package Tracking weight discrepancies Screenshots of the seller admitting the shipment was incorrect Proof of the original order details All communication logs I also requested that the closed claim be corrected and re-opened under the proper dispute category. 

Despite my efforts, PayPal continues to uphold their incorrect determination and refuses to refund the XXXX XXXXXXXX though the circumstances are identical to the previously approved {$2100.00} transaction. 



Financial Harm and Policy Violations PayPal is responsible for honoring its XXXX XXXX XXXX, which states that buyers are covered when an item is : Not as described Significantly different than advertised Missing items Fraudulently misrepresented Not delivered at all All of these conditions apply. 

By failing to uphold their own policies consistently, PayPal has caused me : Financial loss of {$3100.00} Loss of inventory critical to my business Hours of time spent attempting to correct their errors Emotional distress and business disruption It is extremely concerning that PayPal recognized XXXX part of the fraud but continues to ignore the larger payment without valid justification. 



What I Am Requesting From the CFPB I respectfully request the CFPB to investigate the following : XXXX. Why PayPal approved the {$2100.00} dispute but denied the {$3100.00} dispute despite identical evidence and fraudulent behavior. 
XXXX. Why PayPal ignored clear documentation showing the seller engaged in intentional fraud. 
XXXX. Why PayPal refused to re-open or properly classify the second dispute when provided with all required information. 
XXXX. Why PayPal continues to allow a known scammer to keep funds after the fraudulent nature of the shipment was proven. 

I am requesting that PayPal be required to return the {$3100.00} USD that was wrongfully withheld and mishandled. 



Conclusion I have acted in full compliance with XXXX policies. I have provided overwhelming proof of fraud. As a long-time PayPal business XXXX, I rely on fair and consistent enforcement of their XXXX XXXX guidelines. 

XXXX refusal to resolve this issue has caused measurable financial harm. I am asking for CFPB assistance to correct what has become a deeply unfair and unjust situation. 

Thank you for your time and attention to this matter. 
XXXX Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX, Colorado XXXX XXXX

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
In XXXX, my PayPal account was compromised and used to make a series of unauthorized charges, including a {$610.00} transaction that PayPal is still trying to hold me responsible for. 

The hijacking of my account was flagrant and obvious. Someone gained access, changed email and physical addresses on my PayPal account, and immediately began attempting to siphon funds. I did not make or authorize these transactions, and I did not give anyone else permission to use my account. 

As soon as I became aware of the activity, I notified PayPal that my account had been hacked and that these charges were unauthorized. In a written communication on XX/XX/XXXX, I told PayPal very clearly : I did not make these transactions. 
I did not authorize them. 
Someone fraudulently charged to my account. 
Its plain to see someone added different email addresses and physical addresses to my account in order to commit fraud. 
Do not process ANY more payments. No debits to my bank or credit card. Please cease. 

Despite this explicit notice and request that no further payments be processed, PayPal continued processing the disputed activity. XXXX of the key transactions is a {$610.00} instant transfer payment to XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX, which my bank ( XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX declined when PayPal attempted to pull funds from my account. 

I spoke with multiple PayPal representatives by phone at the time and was repeatedly assured that I would not be held liable for charges I did not authorize. I relied on these assurances and believed PayPal would protect me from the fraudulent activity on my account. 

Several charges in this same sequence were eventually refunded as fraudulent. However, PayPal has taken an inconsistent and unexplained position by selectively deciding that XXXX charge for {$610.00} is somehow authorized, even though I reported all of the related transactions as unauthorized at the same time and they clearly form a single pattern of account compromise and fraud. 

PayPal has told me that this {$610.00} payment was processed under a billing agreement with XXXX XXXX XXXX, allegedly created on XX/XX/XXXX. They claim that because this billing agreement existed and some prior payments occurred, the {$610.00} transaction can not be considered unauthorized and they will not reverse it. 

Their own customer service agent XXXX wrote to me on XX/XX/XXXX, stating that they understood I did not authorize this XXXX, that they would cancel the agreement between my PayPal account and XXXX XXXX XXXX, and that they would file a case to help me get my money back. In that same message, the agent referred to the disputed charge as an XXXX XXXX purchase, even though : The transaction is described elsewhere as being to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ; I have never purchased goods or services from XXXX ; and I do not own any XXXX products. 

This inconsistent description of the merchant calls into question the adequacy and accuracy of XXXX XXXX, if PayPal knows that this was used to purchase XXXX products or services, they know more than me. Since I did not ever purchase any XXXX products or services. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, another PayPal agent, XXXX, wrote to me and said that because a billing agreement with XXXX XXXX XXXX was set up on XX/XX/XXXX and multiple payments were made under it, the disputed {$610.00} can not be considered unauthorized, that disputes can only be appealed within 10 days, that each transaction can only be disputed once, and that the case is no longer eligible for appeal or escalation. 

PayPal is therefore refusing to : Properly acknowledge that my account was clearly hijacked ( with changed emails and physical addresses ) and that I promptly reported this ; Explain why some of the related fraudulent charges were refunded while this particular {$610.00} transaction was singled out as authorized ; Reconcile their conflicting references to XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX, despite my never having used XXXX products or services ; Honor multiple assurances given to me by phone and in written messages that I would not be held liable for charges I did not authorize ; or Provide detailed evidence that I knowingly set up this recurring billing agreement or expressly authorized the disputed {$610.00} payment after my account was compromised. 

I am requesting that CFPB assist in having PayPal : Reevaluate their decision and relieve me of liability for the {$610.00} transaction and any related negative balance ; Provide a clear written explanation and documentation of the evidence they rely on to claim this transaction was authorized, including how they justified treating some of the fraudulent charges as unauthorized while denying this one ; and Confirm that they will not send this disputed amount to collections or report me negatively to any credit bureau as a result of this compromised-account incident.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing this complaint because Zions Bank has handled my fraud case with negligence, dishonesty, and a complete disregard for required federal procedures. Their responses have been repetitive, evasive, and clearly designed to protect the bank instead of assisting a victim who lost {$10000.00}. 

From the beginning, Zions Bank has never provided the federally required reimbursement or error-resolution process, even after I repeatedly requested it. They also failed to provide any documentation or evidence that they submitted the mandatory fraud reports to FinCEN, IC3, or law enforcement. To this day, I remain the only party who reported this crime to law enforcement the bank did not.

Zions Bank deliberately misrepresented material facts, including falsely stating that I knew the recipient of the wire. This is an outright
lie. I was in the Zions branch with a bank manager attempting to verify the recipient because I did not know them. Their claim directly contradicts documented events and appears intended to shift responsibility away from the bank. 

Their executives, including XXXX XXXX, continue issuing canned, condescending responses that ignore every legitimate question Ive asked such as timelines, recall attempts, documentation, fraud-report filings, and reporting requirements. Instead of addressing these issues, Zions repeats the same dismissive statements and declares the matter closed, despite failing to follow even basic fraud-handling obligations. 

The bank also withheld critical updates. They waited days before even attempting a recall, failed to notify me of actions taken, and provided no transparency about communication with the receiving bank. Their behavior shows a clear pattern of avoiding responsibility rather than helping a customer who was the victim of fraud. 

No bank should be allowed to operate this way. Zions Bank treated me the victim as if I were the problem. They lost nothing in this incident, yet they have shown no urgency, no accountability, and no willingness to follow required procedures. Their conduct is unprofessional, unacceptable, and deeply concerning for consumer protection. 

Let me be clear : This matter is NOT closed.

I am appealing this further and fully prepared to pursue legal action if necessary. I am also engaging congressional oversight because this situation raises broader concerns about how Zions handles fraud cases and interacts with regulators. 

I am requesting that the CFPB conduct an actual review of Zions Banks actions, rather than relying on the repeated, self-serving statements they continue to issue. Their handling of this case reflects systemic negligence, dismissal of regulatory obligations, and an alarming lack of transparency. Consumers deserve better, and this case warrants meaningful examination.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/2025, I purchased a XXXX XXXX  on XXXX from an XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and paid {$520.00} through PayPal. The seller provided a tracking number through a third-party tracking site ( XXXX ), which later showed as delivered, but I never received the item. 

I contacted the seller multiple times to request proof of delivery, but they stopped responding. I then opened a dispute with PayPal for the item not being received. PayPal denied my claim twice, stating that the seller provided tracking marked as delivered. 

I personally went to my local XXXX XXXX XXXX with the tracking number PayPal relied on. The post office confirmed that the tracking number does not exist in their system and does not belong to XXXX. I also independently checked XXXX and XXXX websites, and neither carrier recognizes this tracking number. This confirms the tracking is invalid and not tied to any legitimate U.S. carrier. 

Despite being informed that the tracking is unverifiable and not from any recognized carrier, PayPal refused to require proof of delivery, such as signature confirmation, delivery address verification, or direct carrier confirmation. PayPal closed my case in the sellers favor solely based on the same invalid tracking information. 

I also contacted XXXX. XXXX stated that my purchase was not covered under their Money Back Guarantee and instructed me to work directly with the seller, who is no longer responding. 

This appears to be a known international scam pattern involving false or aggregated tracking services where delivery is falsely marked without actual shipment. I found multiple public reports from other consumers describing identical issues with this type of tracking. 

Because PayPal refused to reverse the transaction despite clear evidence of non-delivery and false tracking, I was forced to open a dispute with my credit card issuer ( XXXX ), which is currently under investigation. 

I am requesting CFPB assistance to require PayPal to conduct a proper manual fraud review and to refund the {$520.00} that was taken from me in this fraudulent transaction.

Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX and XXXX 2025, I was the victim of an online pet purchase and shipping scam involving the sale of two ragdoll kittens and a fraudulent pet transportation company. 
I was contacted by an individual advertising kittens for sale on a XXXX XXXX XXXX. The seller provided photos and information about the kittens and represented themselves as a legitimate seller. Based on these representations, I sent {$300.00} per kitten ( total {$600.00} ) via XXXX to the seller. The total for both kittens was supposed to be {$1600.00}, the {$600.00} was the deposit for the two of them. 
After payment, the seller instructed me to work with a pet transportation company to arrange delivery. I was directed to a website called XXXX, which appeared to be a legitimate pet shipping and tracking service. The shipping company provided me with a tracking number ( XXXX ) and a tracking page showing the kittens in transit. '' I later learned that this website and tracking system were fake. 
I was instructed to send an additional {$700.00} via XXXX for transportation fees. After sending this payment, I was contacted again and told that the kittens could not be delivered unless I paid a refundable crate rental fee of {$1400.00}. I was sent a document titled Crate Rental Fee Agreement that appeared official but contained false information and a fake shipping company name. 
When I questioned this additional charge, I spoke directly with the individual by phone and specifically asked to speak with a manager. During this call, I was pressured to send more money via XXXX and was instructed to send it to a different phone number than the previous payments. This raised serious concerns. 
The caller claimed the fee was mandatory, refundable upon delivery, and that the kittens were already in transit and also showed proof of a picture of one of them. I was told the delivery could not proceed unless the payment was made immediately. 
These statements were later determined to be false and intended to pressure me into sending the {$700.00}. 
After further investigation, I discovered that the shipping company, tracking number, and documents were fraudulent and part of a common pet delivery scam. No legitimate shipment existed, and the kittens were never delivered. 
In total, I lost {$1300.00} through XXXX payments related to this scam. I have reported the fraudulent transactions to U.S.Bank and attempted recovery however, the claim was denied because the payments were technically authorized, despite being induced through deception and fraudulent documentation. I am filing this complaint to document the fraud, the fake logistics website, the false documents, and the use of phone calls and multiple XXXX numbers to deceive me into sending money under false pretenses. 


With U.S. Bank, I contacted them on XX/XX/XXXX in the morning. They had me explain to them what happened which was stated up above. They did tell me that it does help my case having the pictures saved of the kittens, contracts and conversations. I received the denial letter in the mail on XX/XX/year>. I am contacting them today on XX/XX/year> to see what the next steps are to file an appeal since they never did get any of my evidence yet.

- A police report was filed. 
- An FBI IC3 report was filed. 
- Wells Fargo responded that the LOI could not be honored due to insufficient funds in the recipient account, indicating that the funds had already been withdrawn or transferred.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I am submitting this complaint as a consumer and account holder who has suffered catastrophic financial loss due to a sophisticated and well-documented fraud, and due to the subsequent failure of my banks to respond appropriately, transparently, or within reasonable regulatory timelines. 
Between XXXX and XX/XX/year>, I was the victim of a romance-enabled investment fraud involving a fraudulent cryptocurrency platform operating under the name Onchain Wallet from Growth Labs. Acting under false representations, psychological manipulation, and deliberate deception, I was instructed to transfer funds by wire to accounts represented as legitimate investment destinations. 
As a result, I executed multiple high-value international wire transfers from my XXXX bank accounts held at XXXX XXXX  and Wells Fargo Bank XXXX The total amount transferred exceeded USD XXXX. The recipient bank was located in XXXX. These transfers were induced entirely by fraud. 
Once the fraud was identified, I immediately notified both banks and submitted formal fraud claims. I provided complete supporting documentation, including wire confirmations, transaction dates, recipient details, timelines, and law enforcement references. An Internet Crime Complaint Center report has been filed with the FBI under submission ID XXXX, and a local police report has also been opened. 
Despite this, both XXXX XXXX  and Wells Fargo Bank have failed to provide any meaningful response. To date : I have not received a final determination on my fraud claims I have not been provided with written findings or investigative conclusions I have not been informed whether recalls were attempted in a timely manner I have not been informed whether internal transaction monitoring alerts were triggered I have not been given any explanation as to why international wires of this size, frequency, and destination were permitted without enhanced scrutiny The pattern of transactions should have raised immediate red flags. The transfers were unusually large, repetitive, international, and routed to a foreign jurisdiction known for elevated fraud risk. In my view, the banks failed in their duty to apply reasonable monitoring, escalation, and consumer protection safeguards. 
The continued silence and lack of transparency amounts to unreasonable delay and constructive refusal to address my claims. I believe the banks conduct constitutes negligence and a failure to comply with consumer protection obligations under applicable federal regulations. 
Resolution Requested I respectfully request that the CFPB : Review the handling of my fraud claims by ( case # XXXX ) Wells Fargo Bank Require wellsfargo to provide a full written explanation of their investigations Assess whether failures in monitoring, escalation, or response occurred Determine whether reimbursement is required due to negligence or regulatory non compliance Also on the letter attached you have the contact of my legal representative : XXXX XXXX
